title: Revisited: Want to Get Your Car Handling Better Than Ever Before? Listen to This.
summary: *** WeвҖҷll be taking a break over the Christmas/New Year period and will be back in action mid-January. That means that although there wonвҖҷt be any new episodes for a few weeks, weвҖҷll be taking another look back at some of our favourite episodes.В  *** Are you sick of playing the guessing game when it comes to setting up the suspension and tyre systems in your race or road car? If youвҖҷve ever felt like youвҖҷre throwing alignment adjustments, damper settings, and spring rate changes at the pit wall just to see what sticks when making changes to your or your customerвҖҷs vehicle, then this episode with Bruno Finco of Optimum G is going to be a perfect listen. рҹ‘ү Use the code вҖҳPODCAST500вҖҷ to get $500 OFF HPA's VIP Package: https://hpcdmy.co/podvip Originally from Brazil and now based in Colorado, unusually for a guest of the Tuned In podcast, Bruno didnвҖҷt have all that much interest in cars growing up вҖ” it wasnвҖҷt until he went to university to gain a degree in mechatronics engineering that he joined the schoolвҖҷs Formula SAE team and began a life-long love affair with all things motorsport. Walking out of university and straight into a role at Colorado-based Optimum G, Bruno has since gone on to become the motorsport tech companyвҖҷs Lead Performance Engineer, and now spends his time travelling around the worldвҖҷs greatest race circuits, gathering data, helping both race teams and OEMs optimise their vehicle dynamics, and teaching others how to do the same through the use of Optimum GвҖҷs highly-regarded software packages. The bulk of this admittedly very tech-heavy episode consists of Bruno talking us through the many nuanced points of motorsport handling and performance, breaking down concepts into their many facets. This includes roll centre, tyre slip angle, Ackermann steering, anti-dive and squat, plus much more. title: Drew Wright on modified Series, winch challenges, acoustic matting and flammable P38s
summary: In this week's episode, Martin is at Wright Off Road HQ to chat to Drew Wright about all things motorsport, starting with a look around his very cool 88-inch Series challenge truck which is decked out with portal axles, competition winches, Super Swamper Bogger tyres and loads more trick kit that made it competitive in the winch challenge scene вҖ“ much to the chagrin of owners of newer coil-sprung machines. Drew also imparts his knowledge on his acoustic matting systems, and how the idea of fitting heavy matting to utility Land Rovers makes a big difference to noise levels inside the cab. There's a journey through Drew's Land Rover history, including a Range Rover P38 fire, and a rusty Range Rover L322 which he's considering reviving. As always, we love to get your reviews and feedback вҖ“ send an email to editoral@lrm.co.uk or leave a review on your preferred streaming platform. As always, enjoy the episode.

title: James Pumphrey on Starting SPEEEED: Fear, Freedom, and Finding a New Lane
summary: Send us a text James Pumphrey (Mr. вҖңSPEEEEDвҖқ with three EвҖҷs) joins Mickey Andrade on the Full throtl Podcast for a real convo about building a new channel after Donut, why he wanted вҖңa menвҖҷs style magazine for YouTube,вҖқ and what it feels like jumping without knowing if the parachute opens. They break down the tradeoff every builder knows: making cars nice enough to keep vs building cheap chaos for views. James talks about why he loves the build and break cycle, his E30 obsession, and the wiring disasters that make for the best (and worst) moments on camera. Then it goes way outside the garage: Yosemite backpacking with 1925 vs 1975 vs 2025 camping gear, why the National Park System matters, and how one вҖңdress better without trying harderвҖқ video helped people finally understand what SPEEEED is trying to become. They also dig into DonutвҖҷs lightning-in-a-bottle era, why starting over is terrifying even when youвҖҷve вҖңdone it before,вҖқ and how James got into cars through magazines, early drifting dreams, and the VW/Vortex forum days that shaped an entire generation.

title: Are UK Motorhomes Heading for a US-Style RV Build Quality Crisis?
summary: What can the UK motorhome industry learn from the RV crisis brewing in the United States? In this episode, Matt is joined by US-based consumer advocate Liz Amazing, whoвҖҷs been shaking up the American RV scene with her popular YouTube channel. Liz has helped owners recover millions in refunds after buying brand new RVs riddled with faults, from water ingress and rotting interiors to vehicles delivered without a VIN number. Yes, really. Together, Matt and Liz unpack the root of the problem: mass production, cost-cutting, and a profit-first mindset that's eroding build quality across the Atlantic. And with American giants now owning several big-name European manufacturers, Matt asks the all-important question: could the same thing happen here in the UK? WeвҖҷre also off to the 2026 Caravan, Motorhome & Holiday Show in Manchester, and Matt shares what to expect and how you can get in for free. And in the Q&A, Matt answers your questions on: How to value your motorhome if you're thinking of selling or upsizing Whether that thick line of mastic in your ВЈ100k coachbuilt should be a dealbreaker Tips for keeping your water tanks from freezing on cold winter nights If you own a motorhome, or youвҖҷre thinking of buying one, donвҖҷt miss this honest, practical look at whatвҖҷs happening in the industry and how it affects you. title: First Drive of JasonвҖҷs VR6-Swapped MK3 Cabrio вҖ” Carmudgeon w/ Cammisa & Derek Tam-Scott вҖ” Ep. 221
summary: The day has comeвҖҰ JasonвҖҷs VR6 swap on his Mk3 Volkswagen Cabrio is finally on the road!  вҖҰ.well, sort of. With some frustrating snafus along the way, thereвҖҷs still plenty of work left to be done. But not enough to prevent a first shakedown (and burnout, of course) from happening with the existing stock 12-valve VR6 before it gets replaced by a built motor from Techtonics Tuning. === Visit http://JasonSentMe.com to get a Hagerty Guaranteed Value (TM) collector-car insurance quote!  === Derek and Jason begin todayвҖҷs episode with a recap of the swap progress so far - in a matter of weeks, JasonвҖҷs 1996 Volkswagen Cabrio went from being gutless, to gutted, to glorious - receiving not just an engine swap, but also a full subframe and suspension swap from a VR6 Jetta as well. To top it off, Jason mounted period correct BBS RX multi-piece wheels that really tie everything in together. If Volkswagen had made a true Mk3 GTI Cabriolet, this is about as close as you could get! After discussing hours and dollars spent on the project, Derek circles back on other cars that sound better or worse than a VR6. The Ferrari 512BB comes to mind, even being a Bosch CIS-equipped car. Amongst other CIS cars, Jason also discusses the rich induction sounds of his other cars - including his Mercedes-Benz 2.3-16 Cosworth and Mk1 Volkswagen Cabriolet. And of course, itвҖҷs not a Carmudgeon Show without critically blasting other lesser-sounding cars - in this case, the S14 in the E30 BMW M3 and all Nissan VQ engines. Was all the fuss worth it? YouвҖҷll have to watch (or listen) and find out! Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

title: Ron Ananian, The Car Doctor - January 10, 2026 - Hour 2 - One Chance to Do It Right: Dealer Disputes, Exhaust Surprises, and Battery Timing
summary: This hour, Ron tackles a dealership dispute after a headlight replacementвҖ”when a customer later discovers the entire inner wheelwell liner missing and canвҖҷt prove who removed it. Ron explains how to use factory procedures and documentation to hold a shop accountable and why missing liners can become a real corrosion and safety issue.  Plus: a rare day in the shopвҖ”three exhaust repairs in one dayвҖ”including a Toyota Highlander with a subtle flange leak that snowballed into hardware repairs and an O2 sensor surprise. Then Ron weighs in on oil viscosity вҖңadviceвҖқ from YouTube, a Mazda CX-5 deal for a high-mile commute, and when to replace a battery on a low-use vehicleвҖ”especially with todayвҖҷs electronics and battery monitoring resets. title: Ron Ananian, The Car Doctor - January 10, 2026 - Hour 1 - The Best Repair Is Not To Make One
summary: Sometimes the best repair is the one you donвҖҷt makeвҖ”and Ron explains why with a 2007 Mercury Milan that came in for a simple turn-signal problem. What looked like an easy bulb swap turns into a lesson in modern diagnostics, networked body modules (GEM/SJB), and the harsh reality of obsolete parts and programming limitations.  Then Ron takes a call on a 2023 Chrysler Pacifica with an intermittent no-start and a вҖңpop-popвҖқ sound near the batteryвҖ”plus a hood that wouldnвҖҷt even openвҖ”raising questions about cable routing, connections, possible prior damage, and whether the van has start/stop with an auxiliary battery.  Plus: an F-150 slowly losing coolant with no visible leaksвҖ”Ron walks through pressure testing, dye testing, internal vs external loss, and when a sealant strategy may make sense on high-mileage trucks. And for low-mileage drivers wondering when to change oil, Ron stands by his rule: five months or five thousand milesвҖ”because an oil change is also a maintenance checkpoint, not just fresh oil. title: This Is a STREET-LEGAL Formula Car (Yes, Really) вҖ” RYN Motors Founder EXPLAINS Everything
summary: A street-legal formula car sounds like internet nonsense. Until you meet Caleb Borgstrom. In this episode of Full Throttle Talk, Tim Harris sits down with one of the founders of RYN Motors to tear apart one of the most unreasonable automotive ideas in years вҖ” and why it might actually work. This isnвҖҷt a concept render. This isnвҖҷt a YouTube kit car. This is a real, engineered, road-registrable, formula-style machine built by someone who got tired of being told, вҖңThatвҖҷs impossible.вҖқ In this episode, we get into the uncomfortable questions:    Why Caleb refused to accept that formula cars should be track-only    The exact moment this went from a sketch to вҖңweвҖҷre actually building thisвҖқ    Chassis, suspension, engine, and gearbox decisions nobody agrees on    The hardest engineering and regulatory problems вҖ” and how they reshaped the car    Lap times vs. reliability vs. drivability (something always loses)    What вҖңstreet legalвҖқ really means in the real world    Who should absolutely NOT buy this car    Pricing, customization limits, delivery reality, and long-term support This conversation isnвҖҷt marketing fluff. ItвҖҷs about tradeoffs, consequences, and obsession вҖ” the stuff most founders wonвҖҷt say out loud. If youвҖҷre tired of:    Overweight supercars    Track toys that canвҖҷt survive real use    вҖңDriverвҖҷs carsвҖқ built by committees вҖҰthis episode is for you. title: Saying No To Knock-Offs
summary: Do you know where your parts come from? ItвҖҷs a serious question. One posed recently in a most unusual public confession by Nikola Maletic from Perun Moto, a manufacturer of high-end bits for your ADV motorcycle. Tired of having his parts copied by another manufacturerвҖ”even to the degree that they reused his catalogue photos and descriptionsвҖ”he didnвҖҷt get mad, go online, and lose his mind. How old-fashioned of him. Instead, he solicited the views of the public on what has become an issue of increasing concern: where does the stuff we buy come from? Is it made in a manner that dovetails with my beliefs? Are employees paid living wages? And, specific to the Perun Moto case, do we, as purchasers, have an obligation to honor intellectual property right for those whoвҖҷve invested the time and money to make the bits we buy.

title: More Than Miles: What Happens When Long-Term Motorcycle Travel Ends
summary: In December 2019, Lisa and Simon Thomas left their motorcycles and gear in the United States, planning to return after a short trip home to the UK. More than six years later, those bikes are still there вҖ” and their lives have gone in a very different direction. This episode lives in the space between what was planned and what actually happened. We talk about building a life around long-term travel, what happens when health intervenes, and the emotional cost of stopping after years on the move. Lisa and Simon share what itвҖҷs like to lose вҖ” and rebuild вҖ” identity, confidence, and purpose when the thing that defines you suddenly disappears. And through it all, we explore why motorcycles still matter. рҹ”— Show Notes & Links: Find photos, links, and resources related to this episode on our website at Adventure Rider Radio. рҹҺ§ Subscribe & Never Miss an Episode: We release new episodes of ARR every week on Thursday and of RAW on the 21st of every monthвҖ”full of inspiring stories, expert advice, and insight from the world of adventure motorcycling. title: The Best and Worst Cars This Year вҖ” The Carmudgeon Show w/ Jason Cammisa & Derek Tam-Scott вҖ” Ep. 219
summary: 2025 has come to a close - and itвҖҷs another year-end episode reflecting on the best and worst cars Jason and Derek have encountered this year. Maximum Carmudgeonation is achieved today, so hold onto your hats - and we guarantee, youвҖҷve never listened to another podcast where the Vinfast VF8 and McLaren F1 are both mentioned. === Visit http://JasonSentMe.com to get a Hagerty Guaranteed Value (TM) collector-car insurance quote!  === Before getting into the thick of it, Jason updates us on his MK3 Volkswagen Cabrio VR6 swap - with the 2.slow and the rest of the front + rear subframes out, we learn one other MK3 (Jetta GLX) has been sacrificed in the name of top-down VR6 burnouts. A myth is busted - Harbor Freight plastic carts donвҖҷt appear to be makeshift engine stands after all. But they do explode catastrophically! Derek also goes over some highlights of another year dealing cars at OTS - with sales and consignments including the likes of the Ferrari F50, Porsche Carrera GT, and an array of modern Ferrari Challenge cars (360 Challenge Stradale, F430 Scuderia, and 458 Speciale to name a few). He also reflects on a changing market - moving away from 60s Ferraris like 250 Lusso and 330 GTC. Jason begins with his first wave highlights - including but not limited to: Lancia Stratos, Lancia Thema 8.32, Cizeta-Moroder V16T, Saab 9000 Aero, Alfa Romeo 164 Quadrifoglio, E34 BMW M5 with an S70B56 swap, the Kwiek Classics Mercedes-Benz CLK63 AMG Black Series 6-Speed, Ford Sierra Cosworth, Merkur XR4Ti, Jeep Cherokee, and of course DerekвҖҷs recently acquired Mk1 Jaguar. Derek follows with the Alfa Romeo Giulia Sprint Speciale, RUF Tribute, Kimera EVO37, the Toyota 2000GT, and more recently the Porsche 911 IROC RSR (to be further explored on a future episodeвҖҰ) Jason remarks on many of the the other great cars heвҖҷs driven for various Revelations, Ultimate Drag Race, and Ultimate Lap Battle episodes, including the Chevrolet Corvette ZR1 (C8 and C4), Porsche 992 GT3 RS, Ford Mustang GTD, Ford GT (both generations), W204 Mercedes-Benz C63 AMG (including the Anderzen manual swap), Alpine A110, Audi RS6 Avant, and the Porsche Panamera Turbo S E-Hybrid (the BMW M5 Touring was unfortunately not so good). But not to worry- plenty of Carmudgeonation goes down - with roasts of the automatic Porsche 996 Turbo, BMW i3 and i8, the ND2 Mazda Miata, and even JasonвҖҷs own MK3 Cabrio (while it still had its 2.slow).  All this and more, on this weekвҖҷs end-of-2025 finale of The Carmudgeon Show. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

title: Christmas 2025 extended episode!
summary: Kia have launched the EV5 and their Irish MD Ronan Flood brings us through a record year for Kia in Ireland.  Paddy wants to ask Mark a few things about Christmas FM, including why a particular song is high up on the playlist!! The lads chat to Dr. Becky Spelman, an esteemed HCPC-registered psychologist, entrepreneur, and mental health advocate with over two decades of experience about how music, our mood and many other factors that can determine how safely we can operate a car. What are some of the new models coming to Ireland in 2026 that we should be ready for? Take us on your next Christmas break walk with us and we guarantee you'll get your 10,000 steps in! title: Ludicrous Feed 2025 Wrapped: Full Walkthrough of Electric Car Reviews
podcast_name: Ludicrous Feed | EV Adoption in Australia
summary: Got a Question or Comment? Send us a Text Message! Join Tom and Joy for a comprehensive look back at an extraordinary year for the Ludicrous Feed channel in 2025. This special wrapped episode revisits our journey through a remarkable array of new vehicles, starting with the Deepal S07 and moving through dozens of in-depth reviews including the Xpeng G6, Smart #1, Polestar 3 & 4, BYD Shark, and Tesla Model Y with FSD. We also cover the thrilling MG Cyberster, the innovative Kia EV3 and Hyundai Inster, plus key models like the Volvo EX90, Cadillac Lyriq, and the VW ID.Buzz. The discussion extends to the overseas trips and major international media launches we attended, offering a global perspective on the year's automotive trends. We conclude with our detailed summaries on the state of the market for Mid-Sized, Small-Mid, and Small SUVs, sharing our final thoughts on a transformative 2025 | Video by Tom Gan (Tesla Tom) and Joy of Ludicrous Feed from Sydney Australia December 2025 Ludicrous Feed 2025 Wrapped: Full Walkthrough of Electric Car ReviewsВ  Support the show Catch us LIVE on Wednesday nights 8pm Sydney time on YouTube Contact Ludicrous Feed

title: S11E04: @Fortnine Doc Sends Ryan F9 Around the World!
summary: The legendary Ryan from FortNine drops in to go deep into his most ambitious project yet: a feature-length documentary chronicling a record-breaking, 16-day motorcycle trip around the entire world.  With Zack Courts still out on the mend, Spurgeon Dunbar is joined by CTXP producer Spenser Robert to ask Ryan about his chaotic time in India, the freezing "Nullarbor" of Australia, the logistical miracles worked by Ducati fixers at the Turkish border, and what it's really like to live on a bike for 24+ hours at a time. title: He Expected to be Stopped
summary: In Kevin StaplesвҖҷ mind, there was always a point where the trip would end. He didnвҖҷt expect to get through. The goal wasnвҖҷt success вҖ” it was simply to go far enough to say heвҖҷd tried. To ride up to the wall, touch it, and be told to turn back. So he kept doing exactly that. Showing up. title: The Year Behind And The Year Ahead
summary: This week, Lowdown Show host Neil Graham and ADVrider editor Zac Kurylyk wrap up 2025 with a conversation that ranges across all segments of motorcycling. From Zac's recovery following his bad crash last fall, to Neil's successful return to the racetrack, to the future of Harley-Davidson and KTM, the difference between Dakar and the Africa Eco Race in the upcoming rally raid seasonвҖ”they take a look at every segment of the world of motorcycling in a conversation just like something you'd hear between friends at an off-season riders' meet-up, as they think about the year behind and the year ahead.

title: Are HYBRID Motorcycles Dumb?
summary: Hybrid power can bring the best of electric and combustion power together, but does it make sense for motorcycles? Benda recently showed a hybrid prototype and Kawasaki has already taken the plunge. Technical Editor Kevin Cameron and Editor-in-Chief Mark Hoyer discuss hybrid power, including diesel electric trains and a Canadian company doing similar work on semi-trucks and a diesel hybrid pickup truck to retrofit your full-size bike hauler. Hop on and ride with us!

title: WTF: WHAT THE FLU?!?
podcast_name: The Carpool with Kelly and Lizz
summary: Kelly might sound sick, but it's Lizz's house that has been going through it for the last week. The flu hit her house hard with three ER visits and one 9-1-1 call. Luckily, everyone is on the mend and Lizz is back to tell her tale and give you some good takeaways in case your house gets hit over the holidays. Everyone should be on the mend for the holidays and Kelly is getting really excited for the kids to open all their presents this year. She broke down the ones she is most excited about and let's just say it's going to be a good Christmas in the Stumpe household!В  A quick check in with Industry News has a big story coming fromВ Volkswagen. Could this be the end of the ID. Buzz? title: A Christmas special: talking to Pat Symonds of Cadillac F1
summary: In this week's episode of My Week In Cars, Steve Cropley chats to Pat Symonds, the executive engineering consultant at the new Cadillac F1 team. Symonds, a veteran motorsports engineer who has been involved with seven F1 world championship titles, talks to Cropley about racing cars past, present and future, and road cars, too. It's a long one, so we hope you enjoy it during a bit of down time. We'll be back next week with another festive special, talking to the Duke of Richmond about all things Goodwood. And we'd also love it if you'd subscribe, review and share.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Land Rover Spotting in the Discovery XXXV
summary: In this week's episode, Martin and Steve are heading back to Newbury to pick up Steve's Defender 90 from AllMakes where it's had some repairs after blazing trails at Newbury Showground for the brand-new Big Land Rover Fest. The pair talk modern refinement vs. annoying technology, the styling of the current range and of course keep an eye out for any Green Oval gems along the way вҖ“ and the roads do not disappoint! From all of us here at LRM, have a very Merry Christmas and a Happy New Year!

title: Ep. 9. Christmas Mindset Reset
summary: In this episode, Jason talks honestly about the pressure, the expectations, and the quiet stress that builds as the year comes to a close. This is a reminder that you donвҖҷt have to have everything figured out вҖ” you just have to keep going. A calm, encouraging conversation about resilience, perspective, and finding hope in the middle of the Christmas chaos.

title: End Of The Year Awards, What Do Navy Pilots Drive, Trying To Replace The Wrong Car | Episode 1,026
summary: The guys recap 2025 with their own awards, including Best Car Reveal, Worst Car Reveal, Cars That Surprised The Most, and Disappointments! They first debate choices for retired Navy pilot Mike in NC, who wants a fast, fun-to-drive sports car thatвҖҷs quiet. Then, Chris is in middle America and takes long road trips - whatвҖҷs something comfortable for his wife and Newfoundlands? The third debate is for Bobbie B., who is trying to decide between a G90 and Lexus LS. The guys finish up with Car Conclusions and a wishful future cars discussion! summary: In this special festive edition of the Car Dealer Podcast, hosts James Baggott, Jon Reay, James Batchelor, Joe Wallington and Rebecca Chaplin pick the Car Dealer stories they think were the most important of the year. title: Christmas message from P43 before F1 2026 Rules Explained

title: Eric the Car Guy!! | His Story and Thoughts on Technician Burnout
summary: Like the show? Show your support by using our sponsors.Promotive can help you find your dream job. Touch HERE to see open jobs. Need to update your shop systems and software? Try Tekmetric HERE Wanna go to Tekmetric's first ever industry training event Tektonic? Register HERE In this episode, Jeff Compton sits down with the one... the only.... Eric the Car Guy! His YouTube Channel has impacted millions of technicians over the years and one of those was the host of this podcast...Jeff. Today, they talk about Eric's story from dealership technician to YouTuber, highlighting the setbacks that shaped his career and his drive to empower others. They also discuss the challenges facing today's automotive industry, including the need for mentorship and apprenticeship programs, common misconceptions about flat rate pay, and the importance of valuing technicians as professionals. title: What Really Happens When You Claim on a Motorhome Warranty
summary: Merry Christmas! In this festive episode of the Motorhome Matt Podcast, weвҖҷre diving under the bonnet of something every motorhome owner needs to understand: motorhome warranties.В  Whether youвҖҷre buying a new or used motorhome, this episode gives you a rare look behind the scenes at what really happens when you make a warranty claim. Matt gets exclusive access to Warranty Solutions Group, one of the UKвҖҷs leading providers, now moving into the motorhome and campervan market. YouвҖҷll hear directly from Dennis, a former Bentley claims expert, as he walks us through the end-to-end warranty process, including how a fault is reported, what gets checked, how decisions are made, and how fast claims are paid out. Spoiler: ItвҖҷs not quite what you think. Matt also chats with WSGвҖҷs Group Director, Steph Newbury, about building the UKвҖҷs most customer-focused motorhome warranty product. Whether youвҖҷre a buyer, a dealer, or a repairer, thereвҖҷs something in this episode for you. Plus, in our Q&A, we tackle your questions on: Using campsite WiFi vs 4G internet in your motorhome Streaming TV from your phone while touring Whether itвҖҷs worth upgrading to lithium leisure batteries If you're searching for the best motorhome warranty, wondering about off-grid power, or just want straight-talking advice on keeping your camper protected, this is one to download and share. вң… MOTORHOME MATT APPROVED Services, products and educational resources used and approved by Motorhome Matt mhmp.info/approved рҹӣ’ PRODUCT OF THE WEEK www.thatleisureshop.com рҹӣЎпёҸ 15% OFF INSURANCE mhmp.info/ripeinsurance вқ“ ASK MATT mhmp.info/askmatt рҹ—“пёҸ UPCOMING EVENTS mhmp.info/events рҹ“№ WATCH ON YOUTUBE mhmp.info/watch рҹ’¬ LEAVE A REVIEW mhmp.info/review The Motorhome Matt podcast is brought to you by That Leisure Shop, camping and leisure vehicle accessory specialists.  10% off your first order with the code MOTORHOMEMATT.

title: Peugeot 205 Rallye - A Better MK1 Volkswagen GTI? вҖ” The Carmudgeon Show w/ Cammisa & Derek Tam-Scott вҖ” Ep 218
summary: Derek brings in a rare French treat to the studio this week - a Peugeot 205 Rallye. Take a 205 GTI, replace the fuel injection with Weber DCOE 45 carburetors, remove weight, and youвҖҷve got the key components to the Rallye.Jason, naturally, attempts to make the episode about MK1 Volkswagens - in particular, the Rabbit and the Golf GTI. HeвҖҷs always claimed his 16V-swapped MK1 Cabriolet is the most fun car heвҖҷs ever driven - but can the 205 Rallye dethrone the gold standard?===Visit http://JasonSentMe.com to get a Hagerty Guaranteed Value (TM) collector-car insurance quote! ===...well, yes. Sort of. But first, we have to understand where Peugeot was coming from when they conceived the 205. Derek and Jason discuss the stately and reserved aura of the Peugeot brand before the launch of the 205 in 1983, referencing cars like the 204, 304, 404, 504, and 604. Although the 205 was a modern departure from its stodgy (and abundantly French) past, the base car remained quite frumpy - until the GTI came around, boasting fender flares, rev-happy 1.6 + 1.9L engines, sport-tuned suspension, and a host of other upgrades that made it one of the most iconic hot hatchbacks of the 80s.Jason recalls the time he drove a 205 GTI 1.9 and MK1 Volkswagen Rabbit back-to-back, both owned by Rich Griot of GriotвҖҷs Garage. Having been thoroughly impressed by the 205 GTI, he dives into his latest drive with the Rallye - in comparison to said GTI, a much more charismatic car wanting to vibrate its nuts and bolts apart all the way up to 7,000 RPM.This naturally triggers the inevitable - what criteria makes a hot hatchback great? Is practicality most important, or can great driving dynamics overshadow the initial intent? Jason and Derek reference other small and fun successes like the Hyundai Veloster N, ND3 Mazda Miata, and Subaru BRZ/Toyota GR86 - and how none of them are quite as special as the 205 Rallye.All this and more, on this weekвҖҷs holiday special of The Carmudgeon Show. title: Ep. 310: These Are The 2025 WINNER And LOSER Trucks!
summary: ( https://www.alltfl.com/ ) Check out our new spot to find ALL our content, from news to videos and our podcasts! In this episode of TFL TalkinвҖҷ Trucks, Andre and Roman break down the biggest winner and loser trucks of 2025 after a year of testing, driving, and watching the market react. On the winning side, they talk about why RamвҖҷs Hurricane-powered trucks, FordвҖҷs V8 Coyote, the bold Scout Terra, the often-debated Honda Ridgeline and more managed to stand out in a crowded and rapidly changing truck landscape. On the flip side, they donвҖҷt hold back on the trucks that struggled to deliver, including the continued challenges facing EV trucks, the Ineos Quartermaster, and the ever-controversial Tesla Cybertruck. From performance and practicality to pricing and buyer expectations, Andre and Roman explain what worked, what didnвҖҷt, and what these wins and losses tell us about where the truck market is headed next. title: An ADV Cannonball Run For Anyone
summary: Aaron Pufal is far more than just a man with a grey beard. HeвҖҷs the grey-bearded mastermind behind the ADV Cannonball coast-to-coast GPS rally. ItвҖҷs not like the old cannonball runs, those ones in which youвҖҷd rip through Ohio at 130 miles per hour at 3 am after having ingested a fistful of little white pills. No, this a rally you can do on your oversized adventure bike while following the law of the land. But you donвҖҷt even need a bike thatвҖҷs more than you needed in the first place, because this is a cannonball you can do on your KLR, turning the event into a bowling ball. Just add an extra bungee-cord to your milk crate and off you go. And you can even opt out of the off-road sections and do it on your Electra Glide.

title: What Modern Diagnostics Demand From Independent Garages
podcast_name: On The Ramp - The Independent Automotive Garage Owners Podcast.
summary: Modern diagnostics demand far more than plugging in a tool and changing parts. In this episode of On The Ramp, we talk about what working on todayвҖҷs vehicles really requires from independent garages. The conversation covers process, responsibility and decision making in a world of complex systems, EVs and ADAS. We discuss why fault codes are only the starting point, why structure and method matter, and what happens when diagnostics is rushed or handled without the right understanding. This episode is for garage owners and senior technicians who want to work safely, professionally and sustainably as vehicles continue to evolve. Timestamps: 02:12 вҖ“ SteveвҖҷs background and how diagnostics has changed 06:38 вҖ“ Why fault codes are only the starting point 11:04 вҖ“ Moving from parts fitting to proper diagnostic process 18:27 вҖ“ Teaching technicians how to think, not just test 26:06 вҖ“ Why process matters more than tools 31:42 вҖ“ Charging properly for diagnostics and assessment time 38:18 вҖ“ Intermittent faults and managing customer expectations 44:02 вҖ“ EVs, ADAS and the responsibility garages now carry 49:28 вҖ“ Safety risks and why calibration matters 56:08 вҖ“ Dealer vs independent diagnostics explained 01:02:35 вҖ“ The future of diagnostics and access to vehicle data 01:08:12 вҖ“ Burnout, pressure and working smarter 01:15:48 вҖ“ Raising standards without losing your sanity 01:22:26 вҖ“ Final thoughts on professionalism in the trade This episode is proudly sponsored by Castrol and The Race Group. title: RIDER SKILLS: Why Slow Speed Training Isn't Enough
summary: Slow-speed skills matter вҖ” especially on heavy adventure motorcycles. But if thatвҖҷs where your training stops, there are real situations where it starts working against you. Because sometimes control doesnвҖҷt come from crawling вҖ” it comes from the right momentum at the right time. Clinton Smout from SMART Performance Centre joins us to talk about what happens when the trail gets rough and the bike needs to do what itвҖҷs designed to do: move. title: Episode 18 - John Goss (Part 1)

summary: The Early Years There have been many drivers who've won the Australian Grand Prix, pre-Formula One and many more who've won the Bathurst 500/1000 since the race started in 1960 at Phillip Island but there is only ONE driver who's won both - John Goss! title: 153: A High School Project Gone Wild: 1,450HP IndyCar x Firebird
summary: What would happen if you took an IndyCar inspired chassis, dropped a 1973 Pontiac Firebird body over the top of it, and then added a 1,450hp twin-turbo LS? ThatвҖҷs essentially what Cavan Cameron and his father, Russell Cameron, of Horizon Motorsport have built. The project began as CavanвҖҷs high school engineering project but has since evolved into one of the coolest and most ambitious builds weвҖҷve seen in a while. рҹ‘ү Use the code вҖҳPODCAST500вҖҷ to get $500 OFF HPA's VIP Package: https://hpcdmy.co/podvip In this episode of Tuned In, Cavan shares his journey from a childhood passion for cars to pursuing a career in motorsports engineering, sparked by his fatherвҖҷs involvement in IndyCar. We dive into the вҖңHammerheadвҖқ build that Cavan and his dad have been developing, exploring the challenges of integrating genuine IndyCar components into a bespoke project. He breaks down the technical aspects of the tube-frame chassis, from fabrication and material choices to the complexities of suspension dynamics. We cover the finer details of designing and developing a competitive race car, with a focus on suspension, engine configuration, and aerodynamics. The conversation also touches on the challenges faced during its first outing at World Time Attack Challenge, including managing torque through the transaxle and tuning the car for optimal performance. This build is truly something special, and what Cavan has achieved is seriously impressive. We canвҖҷt wait to see how the car evolves as they continue to refine and dial it in. рҹ‘ү Use the code вҖҳPODCAST500вҖҷ to get $500 OFF HPA's VIP Package: https://hpcdmy.co/podvip Follow Cavan here: Instagram: horizon_motorsports_llc Youtube: horizon_motorsports_llc Timestamps: 0:00 A High School Project Gone Wild: 1,450HP IndyCar x Firebird 4:12 How did you become interested in cars? 5:21 What was your Dads involvement in Indy Car? 10:26 HowвҖҷs the exposure to high level motorsport at a young age shaped your career? 11:53 Is it easier or harder to design parts with a tight set of rules? 16:10 Designing fit for purpose parts 18:58 Tell us about your motorsport degreeВ  29:54 What is the Hammerhead and how did this project come about? 33:33 Did you design the car fully in CAD before starting? 39:27 How did you learn your fabrication skills? 42:56 How do you juggle getting the car done and completing it to a standard youвҖҷre proud of? 45:15 What material is the chassis made from? 50:58 How did you weld the chassis and get it square and straight? 55:11 How does changing the roll centre affect the car's handling? 59:01 Are the IndyCar specвҖҷd fasteners adequate for the extra weight of your build? 1:01:30 How about the components like uprights and wishbones?В  1:06:39 Spring rates and dampers, how did you spec these? 1:15:29 Did you ever consider putting a Cosworth XD engine in the car? 1:18:07 Was it ever a concern that the LS was going to have too much torque for the trans axel? 1:27:27 YouвҖҷre not running any intercooler on this car? 1:32:54 How did you develop the aero package? 1:43:38 WhatвҖҷs left to develop on the car and when will we see it back on track? 1:46:41 Final 3 questions

title: Full Throttle Christmas: One Car from Santa, GT3 Dreams & 2026 Resolutions
summary: $130K budget, hacked Porsches, Bring a Trailer deep dive, and the ultimate holiday вҖңThis or ThatвҖқ Ho ho hold my torque wrenchвҖ”this week onВ Full Throttle Talk, weвҖҷre wrapping up the year at full throttleВ  We start withВ What Did You Do in Cars This Week?В including:    A visit to one of the most incredible private car collections weвҖҷve ever seen (no photos allowedвҖ”yes, itвҖҷs killing us)    Targa Bar, vintage 911 prep, and a real-world discussion about mileage, wear, and why nearly every classic 911 hasВ thatВ gearbox story InВ Automotive News, things escalate quickly:    Modern Porsches in Russia reportedly rendered inoperable via software attacksвҖ”what this means for connected cars worldwide    Porsche unveils the ultra-limitedВ 911 GT3 90 F.A. PorscheвҖ”$400K+, Sonderwunsch excess, Oak GreenвҖ“inspired details, and instant-collectible status    Fiat Topolino and micro-mobility madnessвҖ”are we really headed here? Topic of the Week:A fullВ Bring a Trailer deep diveвҖ”whatвҖҷs hot, whatвҖҷs peaking, and what might already be gone forever. В Christmas Edition: This or ThatSanta says youвҖҷve been good. You getВ ONE car.Total budget: $130,000.YesвҖ”it can be a 911. Carrera T? 991.1 GT3? 718 GT4? Air-cooled Targa? Police tribute SC?We debate. You decide. We wrap withВ listener questions and feedbackвҖ”from 911 credibility earned live on air to thoughtful takes on market segments, Targa values, BMW CSL temptations, and heartfelt holiday notes from the community. В Bonus Segment:OurВ Top 3 Automotive New YearвҖҷs Resolutions for 2026вҖ“ The car youвҖҷre finally buyingвҖ“ The project youвҖҷre finishingвҖ“ The YOLO trip that has to happen Merry Christmas, thank you for being part of this community, and weвҖҷll see you in 2026 at redline. title: THE COMPLETE ORAL HISTORY OF THE CAR MOM
podcast_name: The Carpool with Kelly and Lizz
summary: Lizz is out sick today, so it's all Kelly for this episode of The Carpool podcast! She's getting ready to be the hostess with the mostess for her beauty themed rob your neighbor party this week. Hostess mode is in full effect with the holidays! Speaking of the holidays, Kelly has new notes to add for December including mistakes on the Christmas cards, basics for the kids, keeping ready-made food in the fridge and candy cane fishing.  Then, it's time for a history lesson. A Car Mom history lesson. You may be new to the Car Mom and thinking "How did this even start?" Kelly breaks down the whole story from starting the Instagram page to getting flown all over the country to talk to major car companies. She shares the high and the lows and offers great tips for anyone looking to become an influencer.

title: Jaguar special: we ride in prototype 'Type 00'; interview bosses
summary: This week we ride in the new Jaguar. In the latest episode of My Week In Cars, Steve Cropley joins the team at JLR to ride in a prototype of Jaguar's new electric saloon, which is still under wraps and publically known as 'Type 00'. He also interviews Jaguar managing director Rawdon Glover, and JLR's vehicle design director, Matt Becker. As always there's your correspondence too and a bit of a chat about Matt Prior and Steve Cropley's latest Autocar columns. title: We built an off-road course for LRM Fest! Plus вҖ“ garaging your Land Rover for Winter?
summary: In this episode, Martin and Steve recap on last week's important work вҖ“ building the off-road course for LRM Fest! Martin's Series III picks up some "character", and Steve's 90 liked Newbury so much that it's still there. Elsewhere, the boys discuss galvanised chassis care, and debate whether Land Rovers should be used throughout winter or tucked away for the colder months вҖ“ a subject that's sure to divide opinion! Feature of the Week and Product of the Week are back, and there's exciting news for L322 owners. As always we love to get your feedback and questions вҖ“ email editorial@lrm.co.uk and you could get your question answered on the podcast or star in the mag! Enjoy the episode.

title: Freddie Dobbs: I love the style side as much as the motorbikes
summary: Freddie Dobbs is undoubtedly a very stylish gentleman, and his YouTube videos are a work of art! For him, what you wear on a bike and the look of the bike in question is almost as important as how it performs, and it therefore comes as no surprise that he's a big fan of the British classics like Triumph and Norton. He's also got enormous followers on his YouTube channel and instagram page, so his reviews of the latest bikes on the market is now a full time job for Freddie, as he explains to Iwan and Prutts in this final episode of the series.

title: The Round Table XIV Episode
summary: In this episode of the podcast Gary talks with three senior executives from within the EV industry in the end of season round table episode. Vicky Read is the CEO of Charge UK, the industry body representing the ChargePoint operators Tanya Sinclair is the CEO of Electric Vehicles UK, a body representing the EV industry Vicky Edmond's is the CEO of EVA England, a body representing electric vehicles drivers and advocating for them with government. As you can imagine with such luminaries on the round table the thoughts and opinions were varied and detailed. Amongst many other topics we discussed: - the budget - the role of government vs private investment in the EV transition - the state of the EV market and whether prices are going up or coming down - charging issues and how these are being addressed - and, of course, misinformation in the industry and why it is so detrimental. The conversation covers the roles of various industry bodies, common concerns among EV drivers, the impact of government policies and budget decisions, consumer experiences with charging infrastructure, and the challenges posed by misinformation. The panelists emphasize the need for collaboration among industry stakeholders to improve the EV landscape and address the barriers to adoption. Takeaways The EV industry is represented by various organizations with distinct roles.Consumer perception often lags behind the actual improvements in EV technology and infrastructure.Government policies significantly impact the EV market, but industry-driven initiatives are crucial.Public charging costs have risen, affecting consumer adoption of EVs.Misinformation about EVs can deter potential buyers and influence public perception.The transition to EVs requires a seamless experience for consumers, including reliable charging infrastructure.Emotional barriers, such as fear of the unknown, play a significant role in EV adoption.Collaboration among industry bodies is essential to address common challenges.The used EV market needs to be developed to support broader adoption.Ongoing communication and education are vital to counter misinformation and build consumer confidence. This season of the podcast is sponsored byВ Zapmap, the free to download app that helps EV drivers search, plan, and pay for their charging. title: Can One Metric Define a Driving Experience? вҖ” Carmudgeon w/ Jason Cammisa & Derek Tam-Scott вҖ” Ep 217
summary: This weekвҖҷs episode topic comes from a listener-submitted AMA request - one worthy of its own episode. They ask - what single metric about a carвҖҷs driving experience tells you the most before you drive it? === Visit http://JasonSentMe.com to get a Hagerty Guaranteed Value (TM) collector-car insurance quote!В  === At first, you may think thereвҖҷs one straightforward answer. But youвҖҷd be wrong. Discussion begins with JasonвҖҷs MK1 Volkswagen Cabriolet - a car he claims is the most fun heвҖҷs ever driven. Derek roughly agrees - but counters with the Peugeot 205 Rallye, and much more substantially - the McLaren F1. But how can a nearly 40 year old subcompact convertible be as rewarding to drive as a hypercar? Mass seems like the obvious answer - but a 4000 lb car from today can often out-dance a 2000 lb car from 35 years ago. Take a Lotus Elise (perhaps, with a small cow in it) and a Rivian R1T - both will dance around a race track in a much more similarly than differently vs. any car and truck combination from even 10 years ago. Having covered that base, Jason and Derek explore other possibilities - including peak horsepower per RPM and engine configuration. Derek recalls a time when Alfa Romeo stuffed their Twin Cam four cylinder in everything from GTVs to large Ambulances - simply because it was a such a good engine despite whatever weight it was tasked to pull around. Jason decides to ask AI what it thinks, answering (oddly) with the Toyota Matrix, Honda Fit, and Nissan Versa. On theme with the MK1 Cabriolet, the boys discuss structural rigidity - which leads to another exploration of convertibles ranging from 996 Porsche 911 Cabriolet, to BMW Z3M, Mercedes-Benz SLK, and of course- the Nissan Murano Cross-Cabriolet. ThereвҖҷs an answer for everyone out there - and more on this episode of The Carmudgeon Show. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

summary: Porsche Motorsport legend Alwin Springer reflects on his decades-long career in racing in this 2025 interview with 9WERKS Alwin Springer is a true living legend in Porsche circles. Springer has been called вҖңThe O.G.вҖқ and вҖңMr MotorsportвҖқ by Porsche executives and historians thanks to a 60-year career of high success with both road and racing Porsches all over the US. AlwinвҖҷs incredible, decades-long Porsche story starts in the late 1960s, working on Porsches in Toronto, Canada, before learning the race trade at Vasek Polak in California. From here, Springer helped form the world-famous Andial with two friends, before leading Porsche Motorsport North America and rebuilding it front the ground up. This is a fascinating episode covering nearly 60 years of AlwinвҖҷs life-long success racing Porsche cars, and improving road-going variants. title: Ron Ananian, The Car Doctor - December 13, 2025 - Hour 2 - Stalling Toyotas, Seat-Track Headaches, and Torque Converter Lockup Blues
summary: This hour on Ron Ananian, The Car Doctor, Ron kicks things off with a holiday giveawayвҖ”Car Doctor вҖңhot cocoaвҖқ coffee mugs for callersвҖ”then dives straight into a stack of real-world problems. Paul in New Hampshire calls about a вҖҷ92 Toyota Tercel that occasionally stumbles and stalls after idling; Ron walks through a practical diagnostic path that points toward throttle body/TPS switch strategy and why a lab scope is the fastest way to catch an intermittent glitch. Jim follows with a power seat track alignment issue after a cushion repairвҖ”Ron explains why вҖңthey built it at the factory, so thereвҖҷs a way to re-index it,вҖқ and asks for the VIN to dig up the procedure. Jeff in Oregon brings a вҖҷ95 Dodge Cummins with torque converter lock/unlock hunting; Ron outlines key inputs like brake switch activity and TPS ground noise, and why вҖңmore grounds rarely hurts.вҖқ Brad in Iowa rounds it out with a scary ABS event that wonвҖҷt release the pedal on an вҖҷ09 FusionвҖ”Ron suggests isolating the system by unplugging the ABS module to prove whatвҖҷs good and narrow whatвҖҷs bad. Ron also drops a shop-life reality check on the вҖңsnowball repairвҖқвҖ”when a simple oil change turns into two daysвҖ”plus a quick talk about young techs, presentation, and parents getting kidsвҖҷ cars ready for holiday travel. title: Ron Ananian, The Car Doctor - December 13, 2025 - Hour 1 - Auto Repair Where Everybody Knows Your Name
summary: In this segment of Ron Ananian, The Car Doctor, Ron explains how auto repair has evolved beyond simply fixing cars into a business built on trust, guidance, and long-term relationships. Using a real customer story, he shows how fear of cost or scheduling can lead drivers away from trusted shops, often resulting in repeat visits, unresolved problems, and lingering doubt about whether the repair was done right the first time. That doubt, Ron notes, damages not just one shopвҖҷs reputation but the perception of the entire industry.  He ties this experience to a broader trend of dealership service departments losing ground to independent shops as consumers look for more than a low priceвҖ”they want transparency, familiarity, and the вҖңCheers factor,вҖқ where their name and their car are known. Ron emphasizes that a good repair shop doesnвҖҷt just repair vehicles; it helps owners make practical decisions, including when a repair no longer makes sense. Ultimately, the real value in auto repair comes from honesty, experience, and putting the customerвҖҷs long-term needs ahead of a quick sale. title: Motorcycles vs. The Masculinity Crisis
summary: Ex-Confederate motorcycle designer JT NesbittвҖ”whose current project is re-imagining the American motorcycle with his audacious Magnolia 4вҖ”stepped into the fray recently and wrote an open letter to New York Times bestselling author Scott Galloway. GallowayвҖҷs book, titled вҖңOn being a man,вҖқ discusses the so-called вҖңmasculinity crisisвҖқ affecting young malesвҖ”the very same young males that not so many generations ago were drawn to motorcycling. Nesbitt, whoвҖҷs this weekвҖҷs guest, took exception to GallowayвҖҷs language and suggested a cure that would give disaffected young men their mojo back. And, wouldnвҖҷt you know, it involves a motorcycle.

title: Ep. 309: Are Sport Trucks Making A Comeback? Truck King Weighs In!
summary: In this episode of TFL TalkinвҖҷ Trucks, Andre is joined by Stephen Elmer from Truck King to tackle one big question: are sport trucks actually making a comeback? From modern performance pickups to the brands leaning back into speed and style, they break down what defines a sport truck today and whether the trend has enough momentum to stick around. They also dive into the broader world of truck testing, including events like the Ford Raptor Rally, evolving drive modes, new electric trucks like the GMC Sierra EV, and what really goes on behind the scenes when reviewing trucks. ItвҖҷs an insightful, honest conversation between two veteran truck testers who live and breathe pickups. title: Motorcycle Adventures in Laos - Rally for Rangers
summary: Riding a motorcycle through Laos pushes both nerve and skillвҖ”potholes big enough to swallow a car, sudden water crossings, deep mud, and even the possibility of an elephant stepping onto the trail. A team from Rally for Rangers, led by Steve Zuschin, recently tackled these extreme conditions as they crossed Laos to deliver new motorcycles to frontline Rangers. The ride blended rugged, technical adventure motorcycling with a mission that supports the protection of threatened wilderness. title: Harley-Davidson Big Twin, Knucklehead to Now!
summary: Harley-Davidson debuted the Knucklehead in 1936 and millions of subsequent Big Twins built in its image continue to "potato-potato" down the road! Technical Editor Kevin Cameron and Editor-in-Chief Mark Hoyer talk about the origins of Harley-Davidson's EL and journey through the Panhead, Shovelhead, and up to the modern era. Think of it as the engine that grew with the American highway system! Join us on the ride!

podcast_name: The Carpool with Kelly and Lizz
summary: It's time for a Holly Jolly meter check! Are Kelly and Lizz in that Christmas spirit? Lizz has just started getting her decorations out and had an immediate realization about when the proper time is to put the decorations up. Kelly, on the other hand, is in full Christmas mode! Her gifts are bought and she's making a daily plan of what to do with the kids during the winter break.В  There are some major mindshifts happening with Kelly right now and it's about "blooming where you're planted" and "going deep not wide." What does she mean? It's a new theory she's exploring about cleaning the house and after hearing it you're going to want to change your mindset too!В  In Industry News, Consumer Reports have the best and worst car brands for 2026. Did your favorite make the cut?В  Then it's time to continue the Carpool Podcasts series of interviews on saving money at the grocery store. Today, Kelly and Lizz chat with Morgan of "Morgan Tayler at Home." She's a mom of almost four that started an Instagram page to help other families with a single income make that dollar stretch. She's got plenty of tangible takeawaysВ including protein prep, things you can make from scratch and why you NEED to put a vacuum sealer on your Christmas list!В  To learn more about Morgan's meal plan visit morgantayler.comВ and follow her on InstagramВ @morgantaylerathome

title: Hits and misses of Gerry McGovern, vintage tools and more вҖ“ live from Premier Inn
summary: In this week's episode, Martin and Steve dive into the topic that's been on everyone's lips for the last couple of weeks вҖ“ the departure of Gerry McGovern from JLR. The pair look at some of the big hits of his career with the brand, and wonder what shape the company would have been in without his influence on design. There's also some cracking Listeners' Questions and feedback from the hotel the boys are staying in ahead of Chris McCormack from Oak Ridge Engineering's 40th birthday. Happy Birthday Chris! As always we love to get your questions, feedback and ratings вҖ“ please leave a little review on your preferred streaming platform, or send us anything you like (within reason) to editorial@lrm.co.uk вҖ“ enjoy the episode.

title: Elspeth Beard - The First British Woman to Motorcycle Around the World!
summary: Elspeth is a remarkable lady! 40 years ago, without the reliance on smart phones, sat navs and all of the other global technology we seemingly need today, Elspeth set off around the world on her trusted 1974 BMW R60/6. She didn't really know where she was going or where she'd be sleeping on a nightly basis, but as long as she continued to head west, she'd be travelling in the right direction eventually! It was a life changing experience as Elspeth explains to Prutts and Iwan, who have a sizeable list of questions about her incredible 35,000 mile, two wheeled adventure!

title: Life as a Tech is Not Always "Sunny" with Sunny Massera
summary: Like the show? Show your support by using our sponsors. Promotive can help you find your dream job. Touch HERE to see open jobs.  Need to update your shop systems and software? Try Tekmetric HERE Register NOW for Tekmetric's Tektonic Conference coming up HERE In this episode, Jeff sits down with Sunny Massera. title: Which Cars Are The Best Cars, An Existential Car Crisis, Goth Girlfriends Rock | Episode 1,024
summary: Each year, Consumer Reports publishes their findings on best new and used cars, SUVs and trucks. The guys take the new data and see if CRвҖҷs results matches their experiences, and theyвҖҷre surprised by some discoveries. For Tony S., they debate vehicles that could potentially have a hitch rack semi-permanently installed - but he wants something sporty too. Then, Doug in MI is having an existential car crisis because he needs one do-it-all car for nearly every category of driving. Car conclusions cover an ultimate sleeper for Brian in NY, and a discovery for Paul C., who got a dynamic company commuter car thatвҖҷs also good for track use! title: Ask Motorhome Matt: Towing Laws, Driving Licences and PCP Finance Explained
summary: In this episode of the Motorhome Matt podcast, we're back with the latest edition of Ask MattвҖ”your regular motorhome Q&A, tackling real questions from real van owners. title: We Drove A VinFast VF8 So You DonвҖҷt Have To вҖ” Carmudgeon w/ Jason Cammisa & Derek Tam-Scott вҖ” Ep 216
summary: ThatвҖҷs right - on this episode of The Carmudgeon Show, Jason and Derek risk their lives to deliver crucial mass-market automotive journalism by getting behind the wheel of the VinFast VF8 - one of the most criticized vehicles ever to be sold in the US. But cвҖҷmon, really - is it as bad as everyone says it is? Well. UhвҖҰyeah. ItвҖҷs even worse than you thought. === Visit http://JasonSentMe.com to get a Hagerty Guaranteed Value (TM) collector-car insurance quote!  === Before jumping down the throat of the VF8, Jason and Derek discuss the recent developments of ADAS systems and vehicle acceleration regulations being considered within the Chinese market: вҖңThe cars are too damn fast!вҖқ - CCP, probably. Surprisingly, Jason and Derek both agree - and discuss the potential benefits of having acceleration-limited vehicles, referencing the terror of Dodge Challenger and Charger Hellcat drivers, F80 and G80 BMW M3, and of course the Nissan Altima. As a counterpoint to the VF8, Jason discusses his recent experience driving the newly refreshed Tesla Model Y Plaid, and how he still thinks they are some of the best appliances on the road. Jason was able to test the most recent version of Tesla Full Self Driving (FSD), and how itвҖҷs made great improvements in recent years - even without LiDAR. A great appliance, the VinFast VF8 is not. Jason and Derek share their findings in their individual driving experiences - in agreement on nearly all of the shortcomings of which there are many. At just 10,000 miles, the VF8 has experienced broken suspension components, dangerous throttle calibration, and other poorly-executed features that make us wonder how it was ever allowed to be sold to the public in the first place. There are also dumpsters involved. More on that in this riveting episode of The Carmudgeon Show. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

title: Ep.8 Resilience In The Face Of Adversity (Ciaran Byrne)
summary: On this episode Jason spoke to Ciaran Byrne.  At just 30 years old, Ciaran had already survived three heart attacks. Then came the diagnosis of blood cancer requiring daily chemotherapyвҖ”and most recently, multilevel degenerative disc disease. But CiaranвҖҷs story is about far more than the relentless challenges to his body; itвҖҷs about the resilience of his spirit. Born in County Louth into a family of car enthusiasts, Ciaran grew up in his fatherвҖҷs crash repair garage, learning the craft of restoration alongside his older brother. Those early days shaped himвҖ”but so did profound loss. After the death of his best friend and later his grandmother, the woman he credits as the source of his strength, Ciaran spiraled into a darkness that led to suicide attempts. Surviving them convinced him he was still here for a reason. Today, despite ongoing health battles and the weight of depression, Ciaran is rebuilding more than carsвҖ”heвҖҷs rebuilding himself. With a growing online presence and a return to the hands-on passion of his youth, he shares a powerful message of perseverance, purpose, and hope. This episode dives deep into CiaranвҖҷs journey: the diagnoses, the grief, the grit, and the unbreakable resilience that keeps him moving forward. title: Ron Ananian, The Car Doctor - December 6, 2025 - Hour 2 - (855)560-9900 - Lifetime Transmission Fluids
summary: Lifetime Transmission Fluids, Cold Nissans & Beeping Volkswagens  This week onВ Ron Ananian, The Car Doctor, Ron opens with a nod to The Sopranos and that famous HolstenвҖҷs ice cream shop before diving into the realities behind вҖңlifetimeвҖқ fluids and modern transmission service. A caller with a 2017 Ford Fusion asks about trans fluid intervals, and Ron lays out why waiting until 150,000 miles is a bad betвҖ”and how to approach drain-and-fills vs. fluid exchanges without shocking an aging gearbox.  From there itвҖҷs a full diagnostic tour: a 2015 Nissan Rogue with no heat (airflow but no temperature), where Ron walks through heater-core flow checks, blend doors, and a cheap thermal gun/borescope game plan; a 2018 VW Tiguan plagued by an endless вҖңkeyless entry faultyвҖқ chime and a dealer pushing an $800 module, prompting a blunt discussion of broken door-jamb wiring and the вҖңGerman car experienceвҖқ; and a 2022 Kia Sportage owner trying to sort out backup lamp failures, recall history, and a key Kia bulletin that should keep the repair under warranty. Ron closes with a heartfelt thank-you to the listeners, a reflection on staying вҖңrelevantвҖқ after 30+ years on the air, and a quick reminder that the Car Doctor store at CardoctorShow.com is open for last-minute gifts. title: Ron Ananian, The Car Doctor - December 6, 2025 - Hour 1 - (855)560-9900 - Full Moon Auto Mechanics & The Repairs It Creates
summary: Full Moon Mechanics: Walk-Ins, Walkers & Why That вҖңNoiseвҖқ WasnвҖҷt the Car  This week onВ Ron Ananian, The Car Doctor, Ron takes you inside one of those вҖңfull-moonвҖқ stretches in the shopвҖ”when walk-ins, overdue maintenance, and stacked schedules all hit at once. A routine checklist on MichaelвҖҷs Jeep Compass turns into a full safety catch-up: dry-rotted tires, a worn control arm, driveline fluids, alignment, and more. And the mysterious вҖңclunkвҖқ Michael swears wasnвҖҷt there before? It turns out to be his walker tapping the doorвҖ”proof that not every noise comes from the vehicle.  Ron also tackles listener calls, including a Yukon with intermittent TCM/PCM communication faults, a вҖҷ94 Chevy with a hot-soak slow crank, and the truth about idling a diesel. He closes with advice for retirees choosing their next long-term vehicleвҖ”Pilots, Tahoes, Yukons, Lexus вҖңbrickвҖқ SUVs, and what really matters when youвҖҷre buying the car you plan to live with for years. title: Bonus: Autocar meets Peugeot CEO Alain Favey
summary: Peugeot CEO Alain Favey has a bold plan to put style and driving dynamics right back at the heart of the brand's positioning - and he's joined the Autocar podcast to tell us all about it. Join deputy editor Felix Page at Stellantis's UK headquarters to hear Favey outline why the UK market is so important to Peugeot's EV strategy, how he'll grow its market share in Europe - and how the brand is very literally reinventing the wheel for its next-generation cars. He also told us how Peugeot will rekindle the spirit of the legendary 205 GTi for a new line of hot hatches, and ensure that all future models 'feel like Peugeots', even if they're closely related to cars from other brands.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Tesla texting and driving, Trump kills mpg, Aptera update, and more
summary: In theВ Electrek Podcast, we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this weekвҖҷs episode, we discuss Tesla allowing texting and driving on FSD v14, Trump killing CAFE's MPG standard, an Aptera update, and more. The show is live every Friday at 4 p.m. ET onВ ElectrekвҖҷs YouTube channel. As a reminder, weвҖҷll have an accompanying post, like this one, on the site with an embedded link to the live stream. Head to theВ YouTube channelВ to get your questions and comments in. After the show ends at around 5 p.m. ET, the video will be archived onВ YouTubeВ and the audio on all your favorite podcast apps: Apple Podcasts Spotify Overcast Pocket Casts Castro RSS We now haveВ a PatreonВ if you want to help us avoid more ads and invest more in our content. We have some awesome gifts for our Patreons and more coming. Here are a few of the articles that we will discuss during the podcast: Tesla (TSLA) sales keep crashing in Europe with a single market temporarily saving it Tesla launches вҖҳStandardвҖҷ Model 3 in Europe with aggressive pricing to stop bleeding sales Elon Musk claims Tesla FSD drivers can now text and drive, do police agree? White House unveils plan to raise your fuel costs, despite affordability crisis The Lexus LFA is back, and this time itвҖҷs 100% electric ToyotaвҖҷs $15,000 electric SUV is a hit in China Aptera (SEV) shows off assembly line for solar electric вҖҳcarвҖҷ, updates on battery and testing HereвҖҷs the live stream for todayвҖҷs episode starting at 4:00 p.m. ET (or the video after 5 p.m. ET: https://www.youtube.com/live/poMA4gH3fys
